nameMeanings.name: Cornish
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name for someone from the county of Cornwall, from Middle English Cornish, Cornysh formed on the analogy of English, which was usually Normanized as Corneys. Not surprisingly, the surname is common in adjacent Devon, but it is also established as far afield as Suffolk and London.

variants.name: Corns
meanings: English (Staffordshire and Lancashire): variant of Cornish or Corn , with post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Cornish was born about 1340, in Great Waltham, Essex, England, United Kingdom. He married Joanne Dungay. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in his hometown.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Cornishは約1340年にイギリスイギリスエセックスグレアト ワルサムで生まれた。Joanne Dungayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Cornish(은)는 대략 1340년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 그리트 월섬에서 태어났다. 그는 Joanne Dungay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 그의 고향에서 사망했다.
